title: Maxivision appoints Badrinarayan Srikanth as Group CEO
summary: Former Group CFO takes charge as the eye care network continues its expansion across South and West India
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Former Group CFO takes charge as the eye care network continues its expansion across South and West India

Maxivision Super Speciality Eye Hospitals has appointed Badrinarayan Srikanth as its Group Chief Executive Officer (CEO), marking his transition from the role of Group Chief Financial Officer (CFO).

According to the company, the appointment is a strategic step for the business. With more than 15 years of experience in financial leadership roles and over 20 years of overall professional experience, Srikanth assumes the role as Maxivision continues to expand its eye care network and focus on patient care. During his tenure as Group CFO, he contributed to strengthening the organisation’s financial processes and supported its expansion plans.

Dr G.S.K. Velu, Chairman, Maxivision Super Speciality Eye Hospitals, said, “Maxivision has always been driven by the commitment to provide world-class eye care with compassion and excellence. Badrinarayan’s understanding of our business, leadership capabilities and strategic approach make him the right person to guide the organisation in its next phase of growth. We look forward to his leadership as we continue our mission of transforming eye care in India.”

Commenting on his appointment, Badrinarayan Srikanth, Group Chief Executive Officer, Maxivision Super Speciality Eye Hospitals, said, “I am honoured to take on the responsibility of leading Maxivision at this important stage of its journey. The organisation has built a solid foundation with a clear purpose of delivering accessible and advanced eye care to patients. Our focus will continue to remain on strengthening clinical excellence, empowering our doctors and employees, and creating greater value for our patients and communities. Together, we will continue to build a stronger and more trusted eye care network.”

The company stated that under Srikanth’s leadership, Maxivision Super Speciality Eye Hospitals will continue to focus on clinical governance, strengthen engagement with doctors and employees, and pursue expansion opportunities across its network. The organisation currently operates more than 90 facilities across six states in South and West India.
